en: The mother's answer again shows whither the child's question was really directed. Apparently Anna, too, would like to have a child to "nurse" just as the nurse has. Where the nurse got the little child is quite clear. Anna, too, could get a child in the same way if she were big. Why did not the mother become such a nurse, that is to say, how did she get a child if not in the same way as the nurse? Like the nurse, Anna, too, could get a child, but how that fact might be changed in the future or how she might come to resemble her mother in the matter of getting children is not clear to her. From this resulted the thoughtful question, "Indeed, shall I be a lady like you? Shall I be quite different?" The stork theory evidently had come to naught, the dying theory met a similar fate; hence she now thinks one may get a child in the same way, as, for example, the nurse got hers. She, too, could get one in this natural way, but how about the mother who is no nurse and still has children? Looking at the matter from this point of view, Anna asks: "Why did you not become a nurse?" namely, "why have you not got your child in the natural way?" This peculiar indirect manner of questioning is typical, and evidently corresponds with the child's hazy grasp of the problem, unless we assume a certain diplomatic uncertainty prompted by a desire to evade direct questioning. We shall later find an illustration of this possibility. Anna is evidently confronted with the question "Where does the child come from?" The stork did not bring it; mother did not die; nor did mother get it in the same way as the nurse. She has, however, asked this question before and received the information from her father that the stork brings children; this is positively untrue, she can never be deceived on this point. Accordingly, papa and mama and all the others lie. This readily explains her suspicion at the childbirth and her discrediting of her mother. But it also explains another point, namely, the elegiac reveries which we have attributed to a partial introversion. We know now what was the real object from which love was removed and uselessly introverted, namely, it had to be taken from the parents who deceived her and refused to tell her the truth. (What can this be which must not be uttered? What is going on here?) Such were the parenthetic questions of the child, and the answer was: Evidently this must be something to be concealed, perhaps something dangerous. Attempts to make her talk and to draw out the truth by means of artful questions were futile, so resistance is placed against resistance , and the introversion of love begins. It is evident that the capacity for sublimation in a four-year-old child is still too slightly developed to be capable of performing more than symptomatic services. The mind, therefore, depends on another compensation, namely, it resorts to one of the relinquished infantile devices for securing love by force, preferably that of crying and calling the mother at night. This had been diligently practised and exhausted during her first year. It now returns, and corresponding to the period of life has become well determined and equipped with recent impressions. It was just after the earthquakes in Messina, and this event was discussed at the table. Anna was extremely interested in everything, she repeatedly asked her grandmother to tell her how the earth shook, how the houses fell in and many people lost their lives. After this she had nocturnal fears, she could not be alone, her mother had to go to her and stay with her; otherwise she feared that an earthquake would happen, that the house would fall and kill her. During the day, too, she was much occupied with such thoughts. While walking with her mother she annoyed her with such questions as, "Will the house be standing when we return home? Are you sure there is no earthquake at home? Will papa still be living?" About every stone lying in the road she asked whether it was from an earthquake. A building in course of erection was a house destroyed by the earthquake, etc. Finally, she began to cry out frequently at night that the earthquake was coming and that she heard the thunder. Each evening she had to be solemnly assured that there was no earthquake coming.
zh: 母親的回答再次顯示出孩子的問題真正指向何處。顯然，安娜也想像那位護士一樣，有一個可以「哺育」的孩子。護士從哪裡得到那個小孩，這是很清楚的。如果安娜長大了，她也可以用同樣的方式得到一個孩子。為什麼母親不成為這樣的護士呢？也就是說，如果她不是像護士那樣得到孩子，那她又是如何得到孩子的呢？像護士一樣，安娜也能得到一個孩子，但這件事將來會如何改變，或者她如何在生育孩子這件事上變得像她母親一樣，對她來說並不清楚。由此產生了那個深思熟慮的問題：「那麼，我會像你一樣成為一位女士嗎？我會完全不一樣嗎？」送子鳥的理論顯然已經破滅了，死亡理論也遭遇了類似的命運；因此，她現在認為，一個人可能以同樣的方式得到一個孩子，例如，就像護士得到她的孩子那樣。她也可以用這種自然的方式得到一個孩子，但那位不是護士卻仍然有孩子的母親又是怎麼回事呢？從這個角度來看，安娜問道：「你為什麼不成為一個護士呢？」也就是說，「你為什麼沒有用自然的方式得到你的孩子呢？」這種獨特的間接提問方式是典型的，並且顯然與孩子對這個問題的模糊理解相對應，除非我們假設某種出於避免直接提問的願望而產生的外交式不確定性。我們稍後會找到一個說明這種可能性的例子。安娜顯然正面臨著「孩子從哪裡來？」這個問題。送子鳥沒有帶來孩子；母親沒有死；母親也不是像護士那樣得到孩子的。然而，她以前問過這個問題，並從她父親那裡得到信息說送子鳥帶來孩子；這絕對是不真實的，她在這一點上永遠不會被欺騙。因此，爸爸、媽媽和所有其他人都說謊。這很容易解釋她在分娩時的懷疑以及她對母親的不信任。但這也解釋了另一點，即我們歸因於部分內向的哀傷幻想。我們現在知道，愛被移開並無用地內向的真正對象是什麼了，也就是說，愛必須從欺騙她並拒絕告訴她真相的父母那裡移開。（這到底是什麼，竟然不能說出來？這裡發生了什麼？）這些是孩子插入的疑問，而答案是：顯然這一定是某種需要隱藏的事情，也許是危險的事情。試圖讓她說話並通過巧妙的問題引出真相的努力是徒勞的，因此抵抗對抗抵抗，愛的內向開始了。很明顯，一個四歲孩子的昇華能力仍然發展得太微弱，無法勝任比症狀性服務更多的事情。因此，心靈依賴於另一種補償，即它求助於一種已被放棄的嬰兒期手段，即通過武力獲取愛，最好是晚上哭鬧並呼叫母親。這種手段在她一歲時已經被勤奮地練習並耗盡了。現在它又回來了，並且對應於這個生命階段，已經變得確定並配備了最近的印象。那是在墨西拿地震之後不久，這件事在餐桌上被討論過。安娜對一切都極感興趣，她反覆要求祖母告訴她大地是如何震動的，房子是如何倒塌的，以及許多人是如何喪生的。在此之後，她出現了夜間恐懼，她不能獨處，她的母親必須去她那裡並陪著她；否則她擔心會發生地震，房子會倒塌並壓死她。白天，她也常常被這樣的想法佔據。和母親一起散步時，她會用這樣的問題煩擾她：「我們回家時房子還會在嗎？你確定家裡沒有地震嗎？爸爸還會活著嗎？」關於路上每一塊石頭，她都會問那是不是來自地震。一棟正在建造的建築物被說成是被地震摧毀的房子，等等。最後，她開始在夜裡經常哭喊，說地震來了，她聽到了雷聲。每天晚上，都必須鄭重地向她保證沒有地震要來。
